boundarylogic 0.1.0

    Getting Started with Boundary Logic: Phases 1–3 Boundary Logic with Real Clinical Data: The Pima Diabetes Dataset

    Build a PCA or CVA projection matrix for boundary logic analysis

    bl_build_projection.Rd

    Computes the loading matrix V, its inverse tV, and centering/scaling parameters from the training data. Uses biplotEZ for the PCA/CVA computation and biplot object construction.

    Usage

    bl_build_projection(
  train_data,
  var_names,
  method = "PCA",
  standardise = TRUE,
  proj_dims = c(1L, 2L),
  bl_model = NULL,
  cva_classes = NULL,
  title = ""
)

    Arguments

    train_data

    Data frame with feature columns and a "class" column (numeric 0/1). The class column is used to derive confusion labels for CVA when bl_model is supplied.

    var_names

    Character vector of feature column names.

    method

    "PCA" or "CVA". Default "PCA".

    standardise

    Logical; whether to standardise features before PCA. Ignored (forced to FALSE) when method = "CVA". Default TRUE.

    proj_dims

    Integer vector of length 2; which eigenvector indices to use as the biplot plane. Default c(1L, 2L).

    bl_model

    A "bl_model" object from bl_fit_model() or bl_wrap_model(). When method = "CVA" and cva_classes is NULL, bl_model is used to compute four-class confusion labels (TP, TN, FP, FN) which are passed as cva_classes. Ignored for PCA.

    cva_classes

    Factor of class labels, one per row of train_data. When supplied, takes precedence over bl_model for CVA. Use as.factor(train_data$class) as a simple alternative when no model has been fitted.

    title

    Character string for the biplot title. Default "".

    Value

    A list of class "bl_projection" with components:

    V

    Numeric matrix (p x p); loading / eigenvector matrix.

    tV

    Numeric matrix (p x p); solve(V).

    X_center

    Numeric vector (length p); column means of the feature matrix.

    X_sd

    Numeric vector (length p); column standard deviations.

    method

    "PCA" or "CVA".

    standardise

    Logical; the effective standardise flag after any CVA override.

    proj_dims

    Integer vector; the eigenvector indices used.

    biplot_obj

    The biplotEZ S3 object; used for plotting in Phase 2.

    CVA standardisation

    CVA always forces standardise = FALSE internally, regardless of the value supplied by the user. This is because CVA uses within-class covariance structure that already accounts for scale. The effective standardise value is recorded in the returned object.

    Choosing between PCA and CVA

    • PCA (method = "PCA"): suitable for any data; separates observations by overall variance. Use when class structure is not well-defined in the feature space.

    • CVA (method = "CVA"): maximises between-class separation relative to within-class spread. CVA technically requires at least 3 distinct class levels, but biplotEZ supports 2-class data (results may be degenerate). When bl_model is supplied, the four confusion categories (TP, TN, FP, FN) are used as the class labels, satisfying the 3-class requirement. As a simpler alternative, pass cva_classes = as.factor(train_data$class) directly (2 levels only).

    Examples

    bl_dat  <- bl_prepare_data(datasets::iris,
                            class_col    = "Species",
                            target_class = "versicolor")
# PCA projection
bl_proj <- bl_build_projection(bl_dat$train_data,
                                bl_dat$var_names,
                                method = "PCA")

# CVA with bl_model — confusion labels (TP/TN/FP/FN) used automatically
bl_mod <- bl_fit_model(bl_dat$train_data, bl_dat$var_names)
bl_proj_cva <- bl_build_projection(
  bl_dat$train_data,
  bl_dat$var_names,
  method    = "CVA",
  proj_dims = c(1L, 2L),
  bl_model  = bl_mod
)

# CVA alternative: pass binary class factor directly (2 levels only)
bl_proj_cva2 <- bl_build_projection(
  bl_dat$train_data,
  bl_dat$var_names,
  method      = "CVA",
  proj_dims   = c(1L, 2L),
  cva_classes = as.factor(bl_dat$train_data$class)
)
#> Warning: CVA works best with at least 3 class levels. Only 2 level(s) found. Consider supplying bl_model to use TP/TN/FP/FN labels (4 levels), or use method = 'PCA'.
#> Warning: The dimension of the canonical space < dim.biplot sample.opt method used for additional dimension(s).
